Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar May-28-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion for judgment on the pleadings is ordered off calendar. As defendant points out: "Plaintiff used a fictitious name in his Complaint but does not have approval to do so." (Memo. 1:28; see also Edmon & Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) 2:136.6 ["court may permit plaintiff to proceed under a fictitious name when exceptional circumstances justify protecting his or her true identity"].) Once this gating item is resolved, litigation may resume; until then, it is stayed per the court's inherent powers. (OTO, L.L.C. v. Sup. Ct. (2019) 8 Cal.5th 111, 141.)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
